mnemonics.title: When to use which Scaling
mnemonics.content: MUGS: Min-Max for bounded data (0-1), Uniform distributions; Use Standardization for normally distributed data, Gradient Descent, Distance-based algorithms; Get Robust Scaling when outliers exist.

mnemonics.title: Feature Selection Methods
mnemonics.content: FWE: Filter (fast, statistical), Wrapper (wastes time but accurate), Embedded (efficient, during training).

mnemonics.title: Scaling Formula Memory
mnemonics.content: Z-SCORE = ZOOM: (X - Mean) / StdDev gives Zero-centered data.

mnemonics.title: LASSO Selection
mnemonics.content: LASSO: Least Absolute Shrinkage selects features by shrinking coefficients to Zero.

acronyms.usage: Wrapper method that recursively removes least important features
Feature extraction method creating new uncorrelated components
Measures multicollinearity; VIF > 10 indicates high multicollinearity
Used in Robust Scaling: IQR = Q3 - Q1

acronyms.expansion: Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator
acronyms.usage: L1 regularization for automatic feature selection
Filter method measuring dependency between features and target

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a rubber band stretched from X_min to X_max being compressed to exactly length 1, with X_min at 0 and X_max at 1.
Think of a bell curve being shifted so its center is at 0 and width is standardized to unit standard deviation.
Picture measuring distances using the middle 50% of data (IQR) instead of extremes, making it immune to outliers at edges.
Filter = scanning a menu alone (quick); Wrapper = ordering and tasting each dish combination (thorough but slow).
Imagine coefficients as weights on a balance scale; L1 penalty pushes some weights all the way to zero (eliminates features).
Think of trying to find a specific point in a 1000-dimensional space - the space is mostly empty, points are far apart, and 'nearest neighbors' become unreliable.

analogies.analogy: Just like different currencies need conversion to compare prices, different-scale features need scaling to compare their importance fairly in algorithms.
Like a resume screening process - HR filters candidates based on key criteria (Filter), interviews test combinations (Wrapper), and probation period reveals true performers (Embedded).
Min-Max is like resizing an image to exact pixels (0-255); Standardization is like converting to z-scores for standardized testing where 0 is average.
Having both 'height in cm' and 'height in inches' as features is like having two employees doing the same job - redundant and confusing for the manager (model).
Trying every combination of ingredients to find the best recipe - exhaustive but finds optimal result; Filter is like reading nutrition labels without cooking.
Finding a friend in a small town (few features) is easy; finding them in a million-person city (many features) is like finding a needle in a haystack.
